Title: Widget visible with correct structure on BOPIS-eligible product page
Preconditions:
- Logged in as test user (USER_EMAIL / USER_PASSWORD)
- BOPIS module enabled (xPickupEnabled = true)
- Product with at least 1 pickup location configured
- Desktop viewport (1920x1080 or 1440x900)
Steps:
1. Navigate to the storefront (FRONT_URL)
2. Open a BOPIS-eligible product details page
3. Scroll to the 'Shipment options' section below the Add to Cart button
4. Observe the widget structure carefully
Expected Result:
- 'Shipment options' section heading is visible
- Widget contains a store/pickup icon image (in-store-pickup.svg)
- Widget contains a text link labeled exactly 'Check pickup locations' with a right-arrow icon
- Old static list of store names with inline availability is NOT present anywhere on the page
- No loading spinner shown on initial page load
- Page layout is not broken around the widget area

Title: Widget hidden when product has no pickup locations
Preconditions:
- Logged in as test user
- Product with no configured pickup locations available
Steps:
1. Navigate to storefront
2. Open product details page for a product with no pickup locations
3. Scroll through entire page looking at shipping/delivery options area
4. Inspect DOM if needed to confirm widget is not rendered
Expected Result:
- No 'Shipment options' widget is rendered
- No empty container, collapsed widget, or broken placeholder visible
- No gap or whitespace where widget would have been
- Rest of product page (Add to Cart, description, related products) loads normally

Title: Widget hidden when BOPIS module is globally disabled
Preconditions: - BOPIS feature flag xPickupEnabled=false in store configuration (use store where BOPIS is disabled)
Steps:
1. Open a product details page in a store where BOPIS is disabled
2. Scroll to shipping options area
3. Observe whether the Shipment options widget appears
Expected Result:
- 'Shipment options' widget is NOT rendered
- No broken element, empty div, or JavaScript error related to pickup locations
- Product page loads normally otherwise

Title: Clicking CTA triggers loading overlay then opens modal
Preconditions:
- Logged in as test user
- On product details page with BOPIS-eligible product (TC-4584-01 passed)
- Browser DevTools open, Network tab active, filter set to Fetch/XHR or search 'graphql'
Steps:
1. Locate 'Check pickup locations' link in Shipment options widget
2. Click the link
3. Immediately observe the widget area before modal opens (look for loading overlay)
4. Wait for modal to fully open (up to 5 seconds)
5. In Network tab find request with operation name GetProductPickupLocations
6. Inspect Request Payload to review GraphQL variables
Expected Result:
- Loading overlay (VcLoaderOverlay) appears over the Shipment options widget immediately after click
- GraphQL POST request made to /graphql with operation name GetProductPickupLocations
- Request variables include correct productId and first: 50
- SelectAddressMapModal opens after request completes
- Loading overlay disappears when modal opens
- No console errors during the process
- CTA does not navigate away from product page

Title: Modal opens in view-only mode - no Select button visible anywhere
Preconditions:
- On product details page with BOPIS-eligible product
- Map modal already opened by clicking CTA
- Multiple pickup locations loaded in modal
Steps:
1. Click on any pickup location item in the left-side list panel
2. Wait for location info card to slide in from right (desktop) or appear at bottom (mobile)
3. Examine all buttons and interactive elements in the info card
4. Look specifically in card footer for any 'Select', 'Select this pickup location', or 'Choose' button
5. Check list items for radio-button selection indicators or Select links
Expected Result:
- Info card slides in showing location details (name, address, hours)
- Card footer with 'Select this pickup location' button is completely absent (not hidden, not disabled - not rendered)
- No radio button selection circles visible next to list items
- No Select/Choose button appears anywhere in the modal
- Only available actions are: close info card (X icon) and close modal (modal X button)
- Modal is clearly for browsing only

Title: Radio button indicators hidden in location list (noIndicator prop)
Preconditions: - Map modal open from product page (view-only mode)
Steps:
1. Look at the location list panel on the left side of the modal
2. Examine visual appearance of each row
3. Compare to cart BOPIS modal which has radio circles
4. Hover over one list item without clicking it
Expected Result:
- No circular radio button input elements visible next to any list item (noIndicator=true applied)
- Each list item shows: location name, address line, availability chip - without a radio circle
- No empty space or gap on the left where a radio circle would normally be
- On hover: row shows subtle background color change (secondary-50 tint) indicating it is clickable

Title: Keyword search filters pickup locations within product modal
Preconditions:
- Map modal open from product page
- At least 3 pickup locations loaded
- Note exact name of at least one location before searching
Steps:
1. Locate search input field in modal (magnifying glass icon or 'Search' label)
2. Type partial name of a known pickup location (first 3-4 characters)
3. Click search button or press Enter
4. Observe both list panel and map panel
5. Click X button inside search field to clear
6. Observe list and map after clearing
Expected Result:
- After search: list shows only locations matching keyword
- After search: map markers update to show only matching locations
- After search: map re-zooms to fit filtered markers
- After clearing: all locations restored in list and on map
- If no match: empty state message shown in list (no crash)
- Search input accepts input normally (not disabled during browsing)

Title: Facet filters NOT shown in product modal context
Preconditions: - Map modal open from product page
Steps:
1. Carefully examine the filter/search area near top of list panel
2. Look for dropdown filters or select controls labeled Country, Region, City
3. Compare to cart BOPIS modal filter area
Expected Result:
- No Country, Region, or City dropdown filters displayed
- Filter area contains only the keyword search text input
- No empty dropdown containers or placeholder text where filters would appear
- This is correct: createProductFilterContext() returns empty facets so hasFacetFilters=false hides filter row

Title: Map marker click highlights list item and opens info card
Preconditions:
- Map modal open from product page on desktop
- Google Maps fully loaded with pins visible
- At least 2 location markers visible on map
Steps:
1. Click directly on a map marker pin (click center of pin icon)
2. Observe both info card panel and location list simultaneously
3. Check all visible elements and buttons in info card
4. Click X button on info card to close it
Expected Result:
- Clicking map marker opens info card (slides in from right on desktop)
- Info card displays: location name, full address, working hours (if configured), availability chip
- Corresponding list item in left panel gets highlighted/selected visual state
- Info card footer does NOT contain 'Select this pickup location' button (view-only mode)
- Info card can be closed using X icon button
- After closing info card: list returns to normal unhighlighted state
- No console errors during interaction

Title: Close modal returns cleanly to product page with CTA still functional
Preconditions: - Map modal open from product page
Steps:
1. Click X close button in modal header
2. Observe product page state immediately after modal closes
3. Wait 2 seconds and check DevTools console for delayed errors
4. Click 'Check pickup locations' CTA again to verify it is functional
Expected Result:
- Modal closes smoothly without animation glitches or freezing
- Product page fully intact: all sections visible, no layout shift, no missing elements
- 'Check pickup locations' CTA is visible, correctly styled, and clickable
- Clicking CTA a second time triggers fetch and opens modal again (loading overlay appears)
- No console errors appear after closing
- Page scroll position is preserved

Title: Re-opening modal fetches fresh data and shows no stale filter state
Preconditions: - On product page with BOPIS-eligible product
Steps:
1. Click 'Check pickup locations' to open modal for first time
2. In search input type a keyword (e.g. 'Main') and press Enter
3. Note which locations are shown
4. Close modal using X button
5. Click 'Check pickup locations' again to re-open
6. Observe modal state on re-open: search field content, locations, loading state
Expected Result:
- On re-open: loading overlay appears again (fresh fetch triggered, not cached display)
- On re-open: search keyword field is empty (filterContext.clearFilter() called on close)
- On re-open: full list of all pickup locations shown (no previous filter applied)
- On re-open: map shows all location markers
- No stale state from previous session persists

Title: Modal shows up to 50 locations with no pagination control
Preconditions:
- Map modal open from product page
- Backend configured with multiple pickup locations (ideally more than 10)
Steps:
1. Open map modal from product page
2. Count number of locations shown in list (or scroll to bottom)
3. Look for 'Load more' button, pagination controls, or infinite scroll at bottom of list
Expected Result:
- Modal loads up to 50 pickup locations in a single request (first: 50 in GraphQL query)
- No 'Load more' / pagination control shown at bottom of list in product page modal
- If fewer than 50 locations exist, all are shown
- Total count (if displayed) matches number of items in list

Title: GraphQL response includes all new fields (description
Preconditions: Critical
Expected Result:
- Browser DevTools open with Network tab active (filter: Fetch/XHR or search 'graphql')
- On product page with BOPIS-eligible product
- At least one pickup location has description, contactEmail, contactPhone, workingHours, geoLocation configured in backend
References:
1. Click 'Check pickup locations' on product page
2. In Network tab locate POST request with operation name GetProductPickupLocations
3. Open Response or Preview tab
4. Expand response JSON: data > productPickupLocations > items > first item
5. Check each field listed in expected result
Automation Status:
- Response JSON contains items array with at least one pickup location object
- Each item includes: description (string or null), contactEmail (string or null), contactPhone (string or null), workingHours (string or null), geoLocation (object with lat/lng or string), address block with line1/city/countryName/postalCode/countryCode
- Request variables show: productId matching current product's ID and first: 50
- No errors array in response (or empty errors: [])
- HTTP 200 returned with no schema validation errors

Title: Cart BOPIS modal opens in fully selectable mode (critical regression)
Preconditions:
- Logged in as test user
- At least one product added to cart
- BOPIS/Pickup shipping method available and not yet selected
Steps:
1. Navigate to cart page (FRONT_URL/cart or via cart icon)
2. In shipping method section select 'Pickup' or 'In-Store Pickup'
3. Once BOPIS shipping selected find and click 'Select pickup location' button
4. Observe modal that opens carefully, comparing to product page modal behavior
Expected Result:
- SelectAddressMapModal opens successfully
- Radio button circle indicators ARE visible and rendered next to each location list item (selectable=true in cart context)
- Each location info card (when clicked) shows 'Select this pickup location' button in card footer
- Country, Region, City facet filter dropdowns ARE visible in filter area (createCartFilterContext provides facet options)
- No console errors or inject() warnings in DevTools

Title: Selecting a location in cart BOPIS modal saves correctly and closes modal
Preconditions:
- Cart BOPIS modal open (TC-4584-14 passed)
- Multiple pickup locations available in list
Steps:
1. Click on any pickup location in list to open its info card
2. In info card footer click 'Select this pickup location' button
3. Observe modal behavior immediately after clicking
4. Observe cart page after modal closes
Expected Result:
- After clicking: modal closes automatically
- On cart page: selected pickup location name and address displayed in shipping section
- Cart shows BOPIS shipment with chosen store details
- Selected location persists (refresh page should still show selected store)
- No console errors during or after selection

Title: Cart BOPIS - facet filters visible and functional
Preconditions:
- Cart BOPIS modal open
- Backend has pickup locations in at least 2 different countries or regions
Steps:
1. Look at filter bar above search input in cart BOPIS modal
2. Verify presence of Country, Region, City filter dropdowns or chips
3. Click Country filter and select a specific country
4. Observe list and map after applying filter
5. Look for active filter chip showing applied filter
6. Click filter chip or 'Reset filters' to remove filter
7. Verify list restores all locations
Expected Result:
- Country, Region, and City filter dropdowns (or equivalent filter chips) visible in filter bar
- Selecting Country filter reduces list to only locations in that country
- Active filter chips appear below filter row showing applied filters
- Clicking 'Reset filters' removes all active filters and restores full list
- Map updates in sync with each filter change

Title: Cart BOPIS - keyword search still works correctly
Preconditions: - Cart BOPIS modal open
Steps:
1. Type a known keyword in search input (partial name of a location)
2. Submit search (press Enter or click search button)
3. Verify results
4. Clear search and verify all locations return
Expected Result:
- Keyword search filters results correctly by location name or address
- Behavior identical to before this PR was merged
- Map and list both update on search
- Clearing search restores all locations
- No regression introduced by usePickupFilterContext refactor

Title: Cart BOPIS - reset filter closes info card then refreshes list (behavior change verification)
Preconditions:
- Cart BOPIS modal open
- At least 2 pickup locations visible
Steps:
1. In cart BOPIS modal apply a keyword filter and submit
2. Click on one of the visible filtered locations to open its info card
3. Confirm both filter is active AND info card is open simultaneously
4. Click 'Reset filters' chip or clear-all button
5. Observe sequence carefully: does info card close? Does list refresh?
Expected Result:
- Info card closes first (immediately) - new behavior: closeInfoCard() called before onFilterChange()
- Filter clears: search term removed from input
- List refreshes: all locations shown again via onFilterChange()
- Map updates: all location markers visible
- Both happen nearly simultaneously (info card close then list refresh)
- No console errors
- Confirms new closeInfoCard() + onFilterChange() logic works correctly

Title: Cart BOPIS - load more pagination works for large location sets
Preconditions:
- Cart BOPIS modal open
- Backend has more pickup locations than default page size (typically 10-20 per page)
Steps:
1. Scroll to very bottom of location list in cart BOPIS modal
2. Look for 'Load more' button, 'Show more locations' link, or infinite scroll
3. If 'Load more' exists: click it and observe additional locations loading
4. Check total count indicator before and after loading more
Expected Result:
- 'Load more' button or infinite scroll present when more locations exist
- Clicking 'Load more' triggers additional GraphQL request
- Additional locations appear below previously loaded ones
- Total count (if shown) reflects full count across all pages
- onPageChange callback works correctly (no regression from filter context refactor)
- Pagination behavior identical to before this PR

Title: Full BOPIS checkout E2E - add to cart through order confirmation
Preconditions:
- Logged in as test user
- Cart is empty at start
- BOPIS-eligible product available in catalog
- At least one pickup location configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to BOPIS-eligible product page
2. Click 'Add to Cart'
3. Navigate to cart page
4. In shipping method select 'Pickup' / BOPIS option
5. Click pickup location selector button
6. In modal (selectable mode) select a pickup location
7. Verify location saved in cart
8. Proceed to checkout
9. On checkout page verify selected BOPIS location shown in shipping section
10. Complete checkout or proceed to payment step to verify no BOPIS regression
Expected Result:
- Entire BOPIS cart-through-checkout flow works without errors
- At no point does modal open in view-only mode during checkout (only selectable mode)
- Selected pickup location persists from cart to checkout
- No inject() errors or Vue warnings in console throughout flow
- Checkout can be completed with BOPIS shipment selected

Title: Info card displays all available location details from new GraphQL fields
Preconditions:
- Map modal open from product page (view-only mode)
- At least one pickup location has description, contactEmail, contactPhone, workingHours configured in backend
Steps:
1. Open map modal from product page
2. Click on a pickup location that has detailed information in backend
3. Wait for info card to appear
4. Carefully read all sections of info card
Expected Result:
- Location name shown prominently at top
- Full address displayed (line1, city, countryName, postalCode)
- Working hours section visible if workingHours data exists
- Contact info shown if configured (email and/or phone)
- Location description shown if configured
- Availability chip shown
- Footer area absent (no Select button - view-only mode)
- All text readable, not unexpectedly truncated

Title: Cart info card shows Select button in footer (selectable mode)
Preconditions: - Cart BOPIS modal open in selectable mode
Steps:
1. In cart BOPIS modal click on any pickup location in list
2. Observe info card that opens
3. Look at card footer area
Expected Result:
- Card footer IS visible and contains 'Select this pickup location' button (or equivalent)
- Button is enabled and styled correctly (primary color button)
- This confirms selectable=false hides footer in product context and selectable=true shows it in cart context

Title: Map marker pins use geoLocation data for correct geographic placement
Preconditions:
- Map modal open from product page
- Google Maps loaded with location pins visible
- At least one location has geoLocation (lat/lng) configured in backend
Steps:
1. Open map modal from product page and wait for map to fully load
2. Observe positions of map marker pins on map
3. Click on a map marker and note location name/address shown
4. Verify pin location makes geographic sense relative to address shown
Expected Result:
- Map marker pins appear at geographically correct positions based on geoLocation data
- Clicking a marker displays correct location's info card matching name/address in list
- If a location has no geoLocation data: it either does not show on map or shows at default position (no crash)
- Multiple pins for multiple locations all visible and individually clickable

Title: Network failure during CTA click shows appropriate error state
Preconditions:
- On product page with BOPIS-eligible product
- Browser DevTools open
Steps:
1. Open DevTools > Network tab
2. Set Network to 'Offline' mode using DevTools Network conditions
3. Click 'Check pickup locations' on product page
4. Observe widget behavior when GraphQL request fails
5. Restore Network to 'Online'
6. Click 'Check pickup locations' again
Expected Result:
- When offline: loading overlay disappears after request fails/times out
- Error message or empty state shown (toast, inline error, or modal with error message)
- Page does not freeze, crash, or show white screen
- No unhandled JavaScript exceptions in console (handled error state only)
- After restoring network: clicking CTA again successfully loads locations (component recovers cleanly)

Title: Modal with zero results from keyword search shows empty state
Preconditions: - Map modal open from product page with locations loaded
Steps:
1. In search input type a term that will match no locations (e.g. 'ZZZNOMATCH999')
2. Submit search
Expected Result:
- List panel shows empty state message (e.g. 'No pickup locations found')
- Message is well-formatted, not a blank white area or broken layout
- Map panel shows no markers (or zoomed-out world view)
- No JavaScript console errors
- Clear search / X button available to reset back to all locations

Title: Rapid double-click on CTA does not trigger duplicate GraphQL requests
Preconditions:
- On product page with BOPIS-eligible product
- DevTools Network tab open
Steps:
1. Click 'Check pickup locations' quickly twice in succession (double-click)
2. Observe Network tab for duplicate GetProductPickupLocations requests
3. Observe UI for duplicate modals
Expected Result:
- Only one GraphQL request for GetProductPickupLocations is made (not two)
- Only one modal opens (no duplicate modal on top of another)
- CTA is visually disabled or non-interactive while loading overlay is showing (preventing second click)

Title: Back/forward browser navigation does not break product page after modal interaction
Preconditions: - On product page with BOPIS-eligible product
Steps:
1. Open product page with BOPIS widget visible
2. Click 'Check pickup locations' and open modal
3. Close modal
4. Click browser Back button
5. Click browser Forward button to return to product page
6. Click 'Check pickup locations' again
Expected Result:
- Browser Back navigation works normally
- Browser Forward returns to product page correctly
- After returning: Shipment options widget visible and functional
- Clicking CTA after Back/Forward navigation works (triggers fetch, opens modal)
- No Vue router errors or blank page state

Title: VcRadioButton noIndicator=true hides radio circle element
Preconditions: - QA Storybook accessible (STORYBOOK_URL) OR product page BOPIS modal accessible
Steps:
Steps via Storybook:
1. Navigate to QA Storybook
2. Find RadioButton component in Atoms category
3. In Controls panel enable noIndicator prop (set to true)
4. Observe rendered component

Steps via live environment:
1. Open product page BOPIS modal (view-only mode)
2. Use DevTools Element Inspector on a location list item
3. Look for input[type=radio] element within each list row
Expected Result:
Storybook result:
- With noIndicator=true: circular radio input element has display: none applied
- Label text and content remain fully visible and readable
- Component width/layout does not collapse (no missing space or layout shift)
- gap-2 class correctly spaces content without the circle

Live environment result:
- DevTools Inspector shows radio input with display: none style
- Visually: no circle appears in location list rows
- List item height and spacing are consistent

Title: VcRadioButton default (noIndicator=false) shows radio circle and works correctly
Preconditions: - Cart BOPIS modal open in selectable mode
Steps:
1. Open cart BOPIS modal
2. Observe location list rows for radio button circles on left of each row
3. Click one of the location items to observe visual state
4. Click a different location item and observe state change
Expected Result:
- Radio button circles ARE clearly visible next to each location row (default noIndicator=false)
- When location is selected: radio circle shows filled/checked state with theme's primary color border or fill
- When different location is clicked: previously selected radio deselects and new one selects

- Layout looks correct and matches pre-PR visual appearance

Title: VcPopover content does not overflow horizontally in constrained containers
Preconditions: - Any page that uses VcPopover in a narrow or constrained container
Steps:
1. Identify UI area using VcPopover (e.g. filter dropdown, informational popover, or hover popover in storefront)
2. Trigger popover to open (click or hover as appropriate)
3. Observe popover content area in narrow sidebar or constrained column
4. Check for horizontal scrollbar at page level or popover level
5. Resize browser window to narrower viewport (1024px) and repeat
Expected Result:
- Popover content respects max-w-full constraint and does not overflow beyond parent container
- No horizontal scrollbar appears on page when popover is open
- Popover content is readable (text wraps if container is narrow)
- Fix does not break popovers in wide containers (content still displays normally in full-width contexts)

Title: VcRadioButton changes do not regress other radio button usages site-wide

- Shipping method radio buttons appear and work normally (no regression)
- Payment method radio buttons appear and work normally

Title: Availability chip tooltip shows full description text on hover in product modal

- Map modal open from product page (view-only mode)

Expected Result:
- Tooltip appears (below or above chip, positioned by VcTooltip)
- Tooltip text contains full untruncated availabilityNote text
- Chip itself still shows truncated text (CSS truncate class clips overflow)
- Tooltip disappears cleanly when mouse leaves availability chip

Title: Availability chip tooltip also works in cart BOPIS modal

Title: Mobile 375px - cart BOPIS modal still selectable on mobile

- Cart has products with BOPIS shipping method selected

2. Select BOPIS/Pickup shipping method
3. Tap pickup location selector button

5. Look for and tap 'Select this pickup location' button in info card footer
6. Verify selection is saved
Expected Result:
- Cart BOPIS modal opens on mobile with 'Select this pickup location' button visible in info card footers
- selectable=true prop correctly passed and button rendered on mobile
- Selection succeeds and selected location shown in cart on mobile viewport
- No regression from selectable prop changes in select-address-map-mobile.vue

Title: Tablet 768px - widget and modal display correctly
Preconditions: - Browser viewport set to 768px width x 1024px height
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 768px wide
2. Navigate to product page with BOPIS product
3. Observe Shipment options widget layout at this breakpoint
4. Click 'Check pickup locations' and observe modal
5. Determine whether desktop or mobile modal layout is used
Expected Result:
- At 768px: widget fully visible and not overflowing
- CTA link has adequate touch target size
- Modal at 768px uses either desktop layout (split list + map) or mobile layout (toggle) - either acceptable if usable and accessible
- No horizontal scroll on product page or within modal at 768px
- List of locations readable and scrollable
